titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 570

p.seg: David by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all his bishops, earls, barons, as well as all worthy men of his entire land, greeting.

Know me to have given and conceded to God, Saint Cuthbert, and the monks of the same saint, the church of St Mary at Berwick, with land, teinds, and all the revenues pertaining to the aforesaid church on land and water, and this in exchange for the church of Melrose and for the revenues which they had there, so freely, quitly, and honourably as they ever most freely, quitly and honourably held their church and property at Melrose. Henry my son allows and affirms this.

As witnesses: John the bishop, Herbert the abbot of Roxburgh, William the king's nephew, Ascelin the archdeacon, Robert d'Umfraville, Eastmund the clerk, Berengar Engaine, Waleran the chaplain, Robert Grimbal, Norman the sheriff, William de Sumerville, and Robert de Bournville.
